title: Diocesan Whistleblower Policy
summary: This policy establishes the Diocese of Pensacola-Tallahassee's framework for reporting, investigating, and resolving misconduct—including financial impropriety, harassment, and policy violations—while protecting good-faith reporters from retaliation.
notes: 1) The document is labeled 'Appendix 19,' suggesting it is part of a larger policy manual, but the parent document is not provided; the chapter/section address is therefore speculative. 2) The effective date is inferred from 'Rev 8/14' and the 'Summary of Revisions effective August 2014' header; no explicit calendar date is stated, so the day has been set to '01' as a placeholder. 3) No next review date or review cadence is stated. 4) No explicit records retention period is stated; the 7-year figure is a common nonprofit/diocesan best-practice estimate and should be confirmed against diocesan records management policy. 5) Owner role is uncertain; the policy references 'Pastoral Center officials' collectively but does not name a specific responsible officer (e.g., Chancellor, CFO, or HR Director). A human reviewer should confirm the designated policy owner. 6) The category could also reasonably be 'Risk' or 'Finance' given the emphasis on financial misconduct and fraud; 'HR' was selected because retaliation protection and personnel conduct are the policy's primary enforcement mechanisms. 7) The policy does not reference Sarbanes-Oxley or any specific statutory basis, which may be worth noting for legal compliance review.
